reasoning:
Wesley So is confirmed to be rank 8 on the FIDE November 2025 list. Since FIDE rankings strictly update on the 1st of every month, his rank will not change by November 16, resulting in a near-certain YES. 

However, in the ongoing FIDE World Cup (late Oct / Nov 2025), So was eliminated in Round 2, notoriously resigning in a drawn endgame against a much lower-rated player. This early exit causes a notable drop in his Classical rating (likely losing 4 to 10 points depending on whether the loss was outright classical or he drew classical to lose in rapid). With Gukesh (2763) and Firouzja (2762) closely trailing his 2764 rating and advancing in the World Cup, they are highly likely to overtake him on the upcoming December list, pushing So outside the Top 8.

For the February list, uncertainty increases. He might participate in tournaments like Tata Steel in January 2026 and recover his rating to re-enter the top 8, though the field remains extremely competitive.
